description: Mock/demo EV battery corpus for local CellForge AI evaluation. Synthetic records only; no fabricated DOI numbers.

papers.title: Pulse-rest charging windows for durable LFP fast charging
papers.abstract: Evaluates pulse-rest protocols for LFP graphite cells under 2C to 4C fast charging and reports reduced impedance rise.

papers.title: Low-temperature lithium plating signatures in EV-format LFP cells
papers.abstract: Uses voltage relaxation and differential voltage analysis to detect plating onset in cold fast-charged LFP graphite cells.

papers.title: Fluorinated carbonate additive blends for high-rate LFP cycling
papers.abstract: Studies low-concentration fluorinated additives that form stable interphases in LFP graphite cells cycled at high charge rates.

papers.title: Thermal gradient limits in high-power pouch cells
papers.venue: Mock Journal of Battery Thermal Systems
papers.abstract: Maps temperature gradients in pouch cells during fast charging and links local hot spots to accelerated impedance growth.

papers.title: Rule-based adaptive current shaping for LFP charging
papers.abstract: Compares fixed current charging with voltage-feature-driven current shaping for LFP cells under repeated fast-charge stress.

papers.title: SEI stabilization strategies for graphite under fast charge
papers.abstract: Reviews experimental SEI modifiers for graphite anodes exposed to high-current charging in EV-relevant cells.

papers.title: Silicon graphite blend anodes under aggressive charging
papers.abstract: Explores swelling and impedance changes in silicon graphite blended anodes under high-rate EV charging.

papers.title: Solid-state separator concepts for safer high-power charging
papers.abstract: Benchmarks ceramic-rich separator concepts for thermal stability and lithium dendrite suppression under high current.

claims.claim_text: Pulse-rest charging reduced impedance growth in LFP graphite cells compared with constant-current fast charging.
claims.limitation: Protocol was not tested below room temperature.

claims.claim_text: Longer rest intervals improved capacity retention but increased total charge time.
claims.limitation: Charge-time penalty may be unacceptable for EV users.

claims.claim_text: Voltage relaxation features indicated lithium plating onset during cold LFP fast charging.
claims.limitation: Diagnostic labels relied on post-mortem validation for a subset of cells.

claims.claim_text: Differential-voltage signatures became less reliable when cells had high prior aging.
A fluorinated carbonate additive blend improved LFP high-rate capacity retention.
claims.limitation: Gas evolution was not measured in large-format pouch cells.

claims.claim_text: Additive-derived SEI lowered charge-transfer resistance after high-rate cycling.
claims.limitation: Mechanistic assignment was based on ex-situ surface analysis.

claims.claim_text: Thermal hot spots correlated with faster local impedance growth in high-power pouch cells.
claims.value: 8 C spread associated with 15% higher local impedance
claims.limitation: Cell format and tab geometry affected the gradient.

claims.claim_text: Voltage-feature-driven adaptive current shaping improved LFP cycle life relative to fixed current charging.
claims.limitation: Controller did not include explicit plating diagnostics.

claims.claim_text: SEI modifiers reduced graphite impedance rise under repeated fast charge.
claims.limitation: Reviewed studies used inconsistent electrolyte baselines.

claims.claim_text: SEI stabilizers did not consistently suppress lithium plating at low temperature.
Silicon graphite blended anodes suffered swelling-linked impedance increases during aggressive charging.
claims.limitation: Findings may not transfer directly to LFP graphite cells.

claims.claim_text: Ceramic-rich separators improved thermal stability and delayed dendrite penetration in high-current tests.
claims.limitation: Not demonstrated in commercial LFP EV cells.
